1: GitHub Copilot CLI extends the popular AI pair programmer into the terminal, with its
2: primary advantage being its native, deep integration with the GitHub ecosystem. It
3: understands the context of a project within GitHub. Its agent capabilities allow it to be
4: assigned a GitHub issue, work on a fix, and submit a pull request for human review.
6: 1. Automated Issue Resolution: A manager assigns a bug ticket (e.g., "Issue
7: #123: Fix off-by-one error in pagination") to the Copilot agent. The agent then
8: checks out a new branch, writes the code, and submits a pull request referencing
9: the issue, all without manual developer intervention.
10: 2. Repository-Aware Q&A: A new developer on the team can ask: "Where in this
11: repository is the database connection logic defined, and what environment
12: variables does it require?" Copilot CLI uses its awareness of the entire repo to
13: provide a precise answer with file paths.
14: 3. Shell Command Helper: When unsure about a complex shell command, a user
15: can ask: gh? find all files larger than 50MB, compress them, and place them in
16: an archive folder. Copilot will generate the exact shell command needed to
18: Terminal-Bench: A Benchmark for AI Agents in
20: Terminal-Bench is a novel evaluation framework designed to assess the proficiency of
21: AI agents in executing complex tasks within a command-line interface. The terminal is
22: identified as an optimal environment for AI agent operation due to its text-based,
23: sandboxed nature. The initial release, Terminal-Bench-Core-v0, comprises 80 manually
24: curated tasks spanning domains such as scientific workflows and data analysis. To
25: ensure equitable comparisons, Terminus, a minimalistic agent, was developed to serve
26: as a standardized testbed for various language models. The framework is designed for
27: extensibility, allowing for the integration of diverse agents through containerization or
28: direct connections. Future developments include enabling massively parallel
29: evaluations and incorporating established benchmarks. The project encourages
30: open-source contributions for task expansion and collaborative framework
